results.title: Progress and prospects of innovative coal-fired power plants within the energy internet
results.citation: Yongping Yang et al. (2019) Progress and prospects of innovative coal-fired power plants within the energy internet doi:10.1016/j.gloei.2019.07.007
results.venue: Global Energy Interconnection
results.abstract: Abstract The development of electrical engineering and electronic, communications, smart power grid, and ultra-high voltage transmission technologies have driven the energy system revolution to the next generation: the energy internet. Progressive penetration of intermittent renewable energy sources into the energy system has led to unprecedented challenges to the currently wide use of coal-fired power generation technologies. Here, the applications and prospects of advanced coal-fired power generation technologies are analyzed. These technologies can be summarized into three categories: (1) large-scale and higher parameters coal-fired power generation technologies, including 620/650/700 °C ultra-supercritical thermal power and double reheat ultra-supercritical coal-fired power generation technologies; (2) system innovation and specific, high- efficiency thermal cycles, which consist of renewable energy-aided coal-fired power generation technologies, a supercritical CO2 Brayton cycle for coal-fired power plants, large-scale air-cooling coal-fired power plant technologies, and innovative layouts for waste heat utilization and enhanced energy cascade utilization; (3) coal-fired power generation combined with poly-generation technologies, which are represented by integrated gasification combined cycle (IGCC) and integrated gasification fuel cell (IGFC) technologies. Concerning the existing coal-fired power units, which are responsible for peak shaving, possible strategies for enhancing flexibility and operational stability are discussed. Furthermore, future trends for coal-fired power plants coupled with cyber-physical system (CPS) technologies are introduced. The development of advanced, coal-fired power generation technologies demonstrates the progress of science and is suitable for the sustainable development of human society.

results.title: Journal of Physics: Conference Series: Preface
results.citation: (2025) Journal of Physics: Conference Series: Preface doi:10.1088/1742-6596/3087/1/011001
results.venue: Journal of Physics, Conference Series
results.abstract: It is our great pleasure to present the proceedings of the 2025 2nd International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG 2025), successfully held in Chengdu, China, from June 6th to 8th, 2025. This conference was convened to address critical challenges and foster innovation in the rapidly evolving fields of Power System Engineering and Smart Grids. PSESG 2025 achieved its core objectives: facilitating vibrant discussions on outstanding issues, disseminating cutting-edge research results, promoting the development of applications, advancing disciplinary construction, and nurturing future talent. We were honored to host a distinguished gathering of international experts, scholars, engineers, and practitioners who shared this commitment. The technical scope of PSESG 2025 was comprehensive, encompassing both foundational and emerging themes. Key areas included: • Power System Engineering: Power transmission & distribution systems & equipment, relay protection, automation, power quality analysis & monitoring, system planning & scheduling & reliability, electrical circuits & energy systems, fault analysis & diagnosis, electromagnetic compatibility, hydroelectric systems, system analysis/operation/control, security defense, restoration control, and high voltage engineering. • Smart Grids: Microgrids, Energy Internet, Distributed Grids, Intelligent Dispatch, Electrical Component Networks, Power & Energy Systems, Large Grid Stability Control, Energy Storage Integration, Smart Distribution & Microgrids, Architecture & Design Methods, Wide-area protection/communication/control, Smart Grid monitoring/control, and multi-domain applications. The conference was further elevated by insightful keynote presentations from leading experts: • Prof. Zhang Yiming (Fuzhou University) on integrated technologies for wired and wireless EV charging systems. • Prof. Shen Yongpeng (Zhengzhou University of Light Industry) on high-efficiency, high-reliability control and management of PV-storage systems. • Prof. Zheng Xin (Fuzhou University) on early detection and location of short-circuit faults in AC microgrids. • Prof. Gao Weinan (Northeastern University) on integrated learning and adaptive optimal control for connected and autonomous vehicles. • Prof. Zhang Chaolong (Jinling Institute of Technology) on AI-driven intelligent management and control methods for lithium-ion energy storage systems. List of Committee Member is available in this PDF.

results.title: Preface
results.venue: Journal of Physics: Conference Series
results.abstract: Focusing on the latest research development and progress in the fields of power system engineering and smart grid, the 2024 International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid (PSESG 2024) was held in Beijing, China from August 16th to 18th, 2024. It was presided over by North China University of Technology (NCUT), with the full support of many universities, research institutes and enterprises at home and abroad. PSESG 2024 was created to build a high-quality academic forum to exchange and share successful experiences of innovation in the domains of power system engineering and smart grid, and establish an effective cooperation platform for researchers and scholars from universities, research institutes and enterprises all over the world. Calling for papers on such topics as Transmission and Distribution System and Equipment, Electrical and Energy Circuits and Systems, Power System Planning and Dispatch, Power System Security Defense and Restoration Control, Smart Grid Deployment, Smart Grid and Distributed Energy, etc., this conference attracted about 80 scientists, engineers, practitioners and graduate students worldwide to exchange their current technical application experience and research results, so as to promote the development of relevant fields and industries. The conference took place for three days, among which day two could be deemed the most critical. On behalf of the organizer, Professor Jinghua Zhou (North China University of Technology), first made an opening speech, introducing NCUT and its cross-disciplinary characteristics of energy storage to the experts and scholars attending the conference, and expressing his sincere welcome to the attendees. And next is the keynote speech part performed by several renown experts, including Professor Shunli Wang (Inner Mongolia University of Technology), Professor Zechun Hu (Tsinghua University), Professor Jinghua Zhou (North China University of Technology), Professor Yu Wang (Chongqing University), and Associate Professor Chenye Wu (The Chinese University of Hong Kong). Their speeches not only covered the latest advances in power system engineering and smart grid, but also explored the innovative application of energy storage technology and the trend of the digital transformation of power systems, providing participants with valuable academic insights. Then, the oral and poster presentation mainly presented by young scholars were held to showcase their research achievements. They talked about the technical principles, operational characteristics, optimization methods, as well as challenges and solutions in practical applications of power system and smart grid from different perspectives, stimulating the spark of thought collision and promoting the deepening of academic cooperation. Discussion and Question and Answer happened throughout the meeting. We would like to thank all the authors and speakers for their hard work and wonderful contributions, and all the experts and scholars for their attention and support to this conference. We believe that with our joint efforts, research and application in the fields of power system and smart grid will achieve more remarkable results and make greater contributions to the cause of sustainable development of mankind. Finally, we look forward to meeting again in the next International Conference on Power System Engineering and Smart Grid to discuss the future development path of power system and smart grid. List of Committee Member is available in this Pdf.

results.title: Research on the Development of Smart Grid
results.citation: Qi Gao (2017) Research on the Development of Smart Grid doi:10.2991/EMIM-17.2017.191
results.abstract: With the rapid development of the exploitation and utilization of renewable energy, the world is faced with a series of problems such as how to access and make full use of renewable energy. Smart grid is an effective strategic measure to respond to these challenges. Global research and construction of the smart grid has a very profound impact on the future development of electric power industry. Smart grid is the trend of the world's power grid development, which takes many superior services for tens of thousands of users because of its safety, reliability and efficiency. This article introduces the definition, characteristics, technical connotation and research progress of smart grid and makes some suggestions for the future development of smart grid. The Definition of the Smart Grid With the accelerating pace of global economic growth, rapid development of electric power technology, the increasingly serious energy crisis, the improving power market, the increasing demand of users for electricity services, the phrase “smart grid” came into being. Smart grid is the intelligent grid, also known as the knowledge grid. It is communication technology and a new type of highly integrated grid of power supply and power distribution infrastructure, based on the high-speed two-way communication network and combined with information technology and computer technology. It has such advantages as self-healing, security, controllability, objectivity and fastness. The distribution of China’s primary energy and load center is extremely uneven, which leads to the need for transmission of electric power from power center to load center in a manner of long distance and large capacity with the use of Ultra-High Voltage as the backbone. So our country proposes the definition of smart grid with Chinese characteristics. State Grid Corporation initially put forward the concept of “strong smart grid”, which intends to use UHV power grid as the backbone grid and a strong grid of coordinated development at all levels as the foundation. It will take advantage of advanced technologies of communication, information, and control to build a strong intelligent power grid with features of informatization, automation, and interaction, embodying independent innovation, internationally leading level, and Chinese characteristics. The smart grid can bring benefits not only to the grid, but also to all consumers and the whole community. Research has shown that the economic and environmental benefits brought by changing current power grid into a smart grid outweigh the required investment. From the economic perspective, users can make full use of electricity at less expensive prices during off-peak hours. Total energy consumption will be reduced by involving users in load management. From the environmental protection perspective, smart grid can reduce the amount of carbide emissions by reducing the peak load under the premise of meeting load demand. Besides, new energy power generation has become an important part of the smart grid to replace some of the traditional power and improve the quality of the environment. The Characteristics of the Smart Grid Self-Healing. When a fault happens, the equipment of the smart grid should send a message in time according to the fault type and take the right measures to minimize the loss of the fault brings to the grid. Grid basal system and technical support system is perfect, which could make a good defense for interference and attacks. results.title: Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering
results.citation: D. Danylchenko et al. (2025) Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering doi:10.20998/eree.2025.1(10).333342
results.venue: Bulletin of the National Technical University "KhPI". Series: Energy: Reliability and Energy Efficiency
results.abstract: The article presents a comprehensive study of China's energy security from a technical and economic perspective, taking into account current challenges and strategic goals of the energy transition. The main focus is on the quantitative analysis of structural problems that threaten the stability of the energy system. In particular, based on 2023 data, it was found that the structure of energy consumption remains dependent on fossil fuels: the share of coal is 56.4 %, and oil import dependence is 72.6 %. In addition, the probability of overloading the power grid in extreme weather conditions was found to be 7.3 %, indicating the system's vulnerability to climate change. The study modeled the effectiveness of the implementation of Smart Grid technologies, which increased the efficiency of ultra-high voltage power transmission by 18 %. The study also recorded a 40 % increase in the total capacity of virtual power plants and an increase in the internal rate of return of energy storage systems based on lithium batteries to 8.7 %. From the perspective of long-term strategic planning, the proposed multi-energy flow model estimates the cost of transporting 500 km of green hydrogen at 35 yuan/kg, and also forms a standardized power compensation mechanism (0.08 yuan/kW). The empirical results confirm the feasibility of such measures: the rate of rejection of new transmission lines in the northwestern regions has been reduced to 3.2 %, and the introduction of 12 GW of energy storage systems in the Yangtze Delta has reduced peak load by 15%. It is also demonstrated that the cost of hydrogen production from offshore wind energy has reached 0.3 yuan/kWh, or 35 yuan/kg, taking into account market electricity tariffs. The article proposes strategic measures to ensure energy security, including investments in scientific and technological development in the amount of 0.8 % of gross domestic product, modernization of the power grid with funding of 4.2 trillion yuan over the next 10 years, and the creation of an adaptive political monitoring system. These results form the basis for a systematic electrical engineering approach to ensuring China's energy sustainability in the context of global transformations.

results.title: Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering
results.citation: D. Danylchenko et al. (2025) Research on system optimization and strategic path of China’s energy security from the perspective of electrical engineering doi:10.20998/eree.2025.1(10).333342
results.venue: Bulletin of the National Technical University "KhPI". Series: Energy: Reliability and Energy Efficiency
results.abstract: The article presents a comprehensive study of China's energy security from a technical and economic perspective, taking into account current challenges and strategic goals of the energy transition. The main focus is on the quantitative analysis of structural problems that threaten the stability of the energy system. In particular, based on 2023 data, it was found that the structure of energy consumption remains dependent on fossil fuels: the share of coal is 56.4 %, and oil import dependence is 72.6 %. In addition, the probability of overloading the power grid in extreme weather conditions was found to be 7.3 %, indicating the system's vulnerability to climate change. The study modeled the effectiveness of the implementation of Smart Grid technologies, which increased the efficiency of ultra-high voltage power transmission by 18 %. The study also recorded a 40 % increase in the total capacity of virtual power plants and an increase in the internal rate of return of energy storage systems based on lithium batteries to 8.7 %. From the perspective of long-term strategic planning, the proposed multi-energy flow model estimates the cost of transporting 500 km of green hydrogen at 35 yuan/kg, and also forms a standardized power compensation mechanism (0.08 yuan/kW). The empirical results confirm the feasibility of such measures: the rate of rejection of new transmission lines in the northwestern regions has been reduced to 3.2 %, and the introduction of 12 GW of energy storage systems in the Yangtze Delta has reduced peak load by 15%. It is also demonstrated that the cost of hydrogen production from offshore wind energy has reached 0.3 yuan/kWh, or 35 yuan/kg, taking into account market electricity tariffs. The article proposes strategic measures to ensure energy security, including investments in scientific and technological development in the amount of 0.8 % of gross domestic product, modernization of the power grid with funding of 4.2 trillion yuan over the next 10 years, and the creation of an adaptive political monitoring system. These results form the basis for a systematic electrical engineering approach to ensuring China's energy sustainability in the context of global transformations.

results.title: Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals
results.citation: Tongbo He et al. (2025) Testing Methods for Ultra-High Altitude Environmental Adaptability of Power Transmission and Transformation Sensing Terminals doi:10.1109/ICEEPS66790.2025.11239566
results.venue: 2025 4th International Conference on Energy and Electrical Power Systems (ICEEPS)
results.abstract: As core equipment for grid condition monitoring, power transmission and transformation sensing terminals face critical challenges in ultra-high altitude extreme environments, including material performance degradation, abnormal electrical characteristics, and inadequate operational adaptability. Existing environmental testing standards demonstrate limitations in simulating multi-stress coupled scenarios due to insufficient simulation capabilities and outdated control strategies. This paper proposes an ultra-high altitude adaptability testing methodology for power transmission sensing terminals based on multidimensional environmental coupling loading and dynamic gradient control. Through retrofitting a comprehensive test chamber with integrated ultraviolet irradiation, air circulation, and low-pressure control modules, we established a synchronized multi-environmental factor loading system. The implementation of a pressure-temperature-ultraviolet linkage algorithm coupled with a dynamic gradient control model enables precise simulation of altitude gradient variations. Experimental results demonstrate the method's effectiveness in replicating multidimensional extreme conditions: low pressure (≤70 kPa), intense ultraviolet radiation (≥1000 W/m2), wide temperature fluctuations (-40°C to 70°C), and strong winds (≥15 m/s), while significantly reducing testing cycle duration. This research provides technical support for reliability evaluation of power transmission equipment in ultra-high altitude regions such as the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, demonstrating substantial engineering application value.

results.title: Technical Transformation Scheme and Practice Applied in Ultra-high Share of Renewable Energy Power System at County-level Scale
results.citation: Ping Xiong et al. (2025) Technical Transformation Scheme and Practice Applied in Ultra-high Share of Renewable Energy Power System at County-level Scale doi:10.1109/ICPST65050.2025.11088518
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Power Science and Technology (ICPST)
results.abstract: The rapid integration of variable renewable energy into county-level power grids has introduced significant operational challenges, particularly transmission congestion and transformer overload caused by bi-directional flow. To overcome the restriction of conventional grid reinforcement solutions focused mainly on infrastructure expansion, the work develops an integrated generation-grid-load-storage coordination framework adapted to scenarios with high penetration of renewables. The solution achieves localized energy balance through optimized energy storage allocation and operational modes control, establishing a resilient power grid architecture capable of grid-connected interaction and autonomous islanded operation. Renovation schemes of building a power system with ultra-high renewable penetration ratios are presented, in which technical routes for the coordination framework are investigated. Performance of key technologies, including grid-forming voltage source converters, smooth mode transition, and the collective control of the coordination system in support of the independent operation and stability of a 100% variable inverter-based renewable energy sources power system, is validated.

results.title: Implications of GB/T 22384 Revision for Standardized Testing of Power Grid Security and Stability Control Systems
results.citation: Lei Fu et al. (2025) Implications of GB/T 22384 Revision for Standardized Testing of Power Grid Security and Stability Control Systems doi:10.1109/ICPST65050.2025.11089103
results.venue: 2025 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Power Science and Technology (ICPST)
results.abstract: The revision of China’s national standard GB/T 22384 addresses critical challenges posed by new-type power grids, characterized by large-scale renewable energy integration, ultra-high voltage AC/DC transmission, and cyber-physical system complexities. Aligned with China’s "dual carbon" strategy and the technical demands of New Power Systems, this revision introduces expanded testing scopes, enhanced network security protection requirements, and refined dynamic simulation methodologies. A case study of the Qing-Yu HVDC project demonstrates the effectiveness of closed-loop test based on real-time digital simulation (RTDS) in validating hierarchical security and stability control systems (SSCS), revealing latent defects such as redundant tripping commands during cascading faults. Innovations lie in integrating RTDS for closed-loop validation of multi-scenario strategies, introducing dynamic state synchronization to mitigate coordination failures, and optimizing lifecycle testing management. The revised standard establishes a robust framework for standardized testing, ensuring reliability and adaptability of SSCS in high-renewable, power electronics-dominated grids. From the main aspects of this revision and the results of engineering empirical tests, the testing methods and technical requirements for SSCS should evolve iteratively in sync with the development process and technological advancements of power systems. SSCS plays a crucial role in ensuring the secure and stable operation of power systems, and the standardization work cannot be achieved just in one effort.

Aligned with China’s "dual carbon" strategy and the technical demands of New Power Systems, this revision introduces expanded testing scopes, enhanced network security protection requirements, and refined dynamic simulation methodologies. A case study of the Qing-Yu HVDC project demonstrates the effectiveness of closed-loop test based on real-time digital simulation (RTDS) in validating hierarchical security and stability control systems (SSCS), revealing latent defects such as redundant tripping commands during cascading faults. Innovations lie in integrating RTDS for closed-loop validation of multi-scenario strategies, introducing dynamic state synchronization to mitigate coordination failures, and optimizing lifecycle testing management. The revised standard establishes a robust framework for standardized testing, ensuring reliability and adaptability of SSCS in high-renewable, power electronics-dominated grids. From the main aspects of this revision and the results of engineering empirical tests, the testing methods and technical requirements for SSCS should evolve iteratively in sync with the development process and technological advancements of power systems. SSCS plays a crucial role in ensuring the secure and stable operation of power systems, and the standardization work cannot be achieved just in one effort.

results.title: Analysis and Evaluation Methods for UHV AC Retrofitting Schemes and Application Effects under Renewable Energy Transmission Scenarios
results.citation: H. Cai et al. (2026) Analysis and Evaluation Methods for UHV AC Retrofitting Schemes and Application Effects under Renewable Energy Transmission Scenarios doi:10.32604/ee.2026.081805
results.venue: Energy Engineering
results.abstract: : Aiming at the challenge of large-scale centralized integration of high-proportion renewable energy, and addressing current bottlenecks in ultra-high voltage (UHV) AC power grids—such as low utilization rates of transmission corridors and poor active power flow controllability—this paper proposes various upgrading and transformation schemes for UHV AC ring networks. These schemes aim to simultaneously enhance the renewable energy accommodation and operational regulation capabilities of the AC grid. First, targeting typical UHV double-circuit transmission corridors, three topological schemes are designed: direct AC integration of renewable energy, single-circuit voltage source converter-based high voltage direct current (VSC-HVDC) transformation, and double-circuit full VSC-HVDC transformation. Second, a unified DC power flow mathematical model is established to theoretically derive and reveal the power flow control characteristics under traditional AC integration, single-circuit VSC-HVDC transformation, and full VSC-HVDC transformation. Finally, a multi-dimensional evaluation system is constructed, encompassing power flow control flexibility, static and transient voltage stability, and short-circuit current levels. Simulations and analyses are then conducted using the Power System Dispatcher-Based Performance Analysis (PSD-BPA) program based on the 2040 planning data for the East China Power Grid. The research results indicate that the double-circuit full VSC-HVDC transformation scheme can overcome the physical constraints of the AC grid’s natural impedance, achieving active regulation of transmission power. Simultaneously, it effectively limits short-circuit currents by leveraging its nonlinear high-impedance characteristics. This scheme serves as an effective technical pathway for resolving the challenges of high-proportion renewable energy transmission and ensuring the safe and stable operation of receiving-end power grids.

results.title: Exact solutions and dynamical analysis of nonlinear electric transmission network via second-kind Jacobi elliptic function
results.citation: Peng Guo et al. (2025) Exact solutions and dynamical analysis of nonlinear electric transmission network via second-kind Jacobi elliptic function doi:10.1063/5.0296345
results.venue: AIP Advances
results.abstract: This paper focuses on the study of a Nonlinear Electric Transmission Network (NETN). Addressing key engineering challenges such as modulation instability, insufficient numerical calculation accuracy, and limitations in system stability evaluation, it proposes three core innovations and clarifies their practical application value. First, by employing the second-kind Jacobi elliptic equation method, a large number of Jacobi elliptic voltage solutions with double-periodic characteristics are discovered in the modeling of complex high-order circuit transmission line differential equations. These solutions can accurately characterize the complex variation patterns of voltage physical quantities, providing a basis for predicting voltage fluctuations in the power supply systems of key facilities (e.g., communication base stations and core computer rooms). This effectively alleviates signal distortion caused by modulation instability, enhances the reliability of communication signal transmission, and reduces data error rates. Second, the equation is transformed into a unique dynamical structure, and a fourth-order NETN model is constructed to explore the three-dimensional dynamical system, phase portraits, and Lyapunov exponent. Breaking through the limitations of traditional second-order models, this model provides a more precise framework for system stability evaluation, enabling early identification of instability risks (e.g., equipment damage caused by harmonics) during power transmission and avoiding unexpected communication interruptions and economic losses. Third, the six-stage, fifth-order Kutta–Nyström method is innovatively applied to the numerical calculation of NETN, significantly improving calculation accuracy. This provides efficient support for circuit parameter optimization (e.g., adjusting the dispersive element Cs to balance dispersion and nonlinear effects), which not only reduces power loss caused by impure signal spectra but also helps lower carbon emissions of the power grid, aligning with the energy-efficient operation requirements of modern power systems. These innovations fill the research gaps related to NETN and provide key technical support for the engineering optimization and stable operation of nonlinear transmission networks.
